content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 25,
1994
Judith Bruno
Executive Property Manager
Western Care Construction Company,
Inc.
4020 Sierra College Blvd.,
#200
Rocklin,
CA 95677
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1—93-475
Dear Ms.
Bruno:
You have requested advice concerning the óampaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act “the Act”.!
Because your
questions are general in nature,
we consider your letter to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c) (3)
2/
QUESTIONS
If a major donor committee makes a personal loan to a husband
and wife and one of them runs for office after the loan is
consummated,
you have asked if the loan is required to be reported
on the entity’s Form 461 in any of the following circumstances:
1.
If the loan proceeds will be used by the borrowers for
personal expenses.
2.
If you do not know how the loan proceeds will be used.
3.
If the borrowers subsequently lend the proceeds to the
spouse’s campaign committee.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Ms.
Bruno
Our File No.
1—93-475
Page Two
CONCLUSIONS
1-2.
A loan is not required to belisc1osed if it is clear
from surrounding circumstances that it was made for personal
purposes unrelated to the recipient’s candidacy or,status as an
officeholder.
3.
A loan made to a candidate is required to be disclosed
unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the loan
was received from a commercial lending institution in the ordinary
course of business, or it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it was not made for political purposes.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires persons or combinations of persons who
contribute $10,000 or more in a calendar year to state and local
candidates and committees to file periodic campaign reports
disclosing contributions and expenditures made.
(Sections
82013(c),
84200—8.4216.5.)
In Section 84216, the Act states that:
a loan received by a candidate or committee is a
contribution unless the loan is received from a
commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of
business, or it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes.
Additionally, .Regulation 18215 states in part that:
A contribution is any monetary or nonmonetary
payment made for political purposes for which full and
adequate consideration is not made to the donor.
A
payment is made for political purposes if it is
received by or made at the behest of a candidate, unless
it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the
payment was received or made at his or her behest for
personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or
status as an office holder.
Your request for advice does not contain sufficient
information to determine if the loan in question will be made for
political purposes.
Some factors to be considered in making this
determination would include:
(1) the terms and conditions of the
loan;
(2) the timing of the loan;
(3)
whether at the time of
making the loan the entity knew that the recipient was a candidate
or intended to run for office;
(4) whether the entity has made
personal loans to individuals in the past;
and
(5)
whether the
loan proceeds were used in a political campaign.
Ultimately, the
question is whether the entity made the loan for political or
purely commercial purposes.


Ms.
Bruno
Our File No.
1—93-475
Page Three
If the loan was made for political purposes, the entity must
report the loan on the campaign statement
(Form 461)
covering the
period during which the loan was made.
(Section 84216(b).)
Pursuant to your request, copies of the references cited in
this letter are enclosed.
If you have any questiQns or need
further information, please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
